rationale: This page contains a high-quality comparison table between nuclear fission and fusion, along with specific details about the ITER project and India's contributions. This is excellent material for technical and current affairs questions in science and technology.
cleanedText:
7. ALTERNATIVE ENERGY
7.1. NUCLEAR ENERGY
Concept Corner: Nuclear Fission and Fusion
Comparison between Nuclear Fission and Fusion
| Parameter | Fission | Fusion |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Process | Fission occurs when a neutron slams into a larger atom, forcing it to excite and split into two smaller atoms. Additional neutrons are also released that can initiate a chain reaction. | Fusion occurs when two atoms slam together to form a heavier atom, like when two hydrogen atoms fuse to form one helium atom. |
| Fuel | Uranium, plutonium etc. | Generally two hydrogen (H) isotopes- deuterium (D) and tritium (T). |
| Energy Production | Less energy per kilogram of fuel than fusion | Fusion could generate four times more energy per kilogram of fuel than fission |
| Energy requirement | Requires less energy to split two atoms in a fission reaction | To achieve fusion, the plasma in the tokamak must reach 150,000,000 °C |
| Technology and Application | The technology of nuclear fission is mature and has a proven track record. | Still at the experimental stage |
| Waste | Waste has unstable nuclei; some of these are radioactive for millions of years. Such as iodine, caesium, strontium, xenon etc. | Fusion on the other hand does not create any long-lived radioactive nuclear waste. Only helium and neutrons. |
| Occurrence | Does not occur naturally on Earth; needs reactors. | Happens naturally in stars (e.g., the sun). |
NOTE: Net Energy Gain (NEG) is critical for commercial fusion power, which implies nuclear fusion process generates more energy than the nuclear fusion process consumes.
7.1.1. TOKAMAK REACTORS
Why in the News?
World’s biggest nuclear fusion project, ITER has completed its central magnet system, with India playing a critical role in building several key components.
More on the News
- India has manufactured cryostat, an enormous chamber that houses the entire ITER Tokamak.
- India has also built cryolines that carry liquid helium to cool magnets.
About ITER
- Objective: To demonstrate the viability of fusion as an abundant, safe, carbon-free energy source.
- Located in France.
- India’s Membership: Institute for Plasma Research in Ahmedabad is lead institution from India participating in the project.
